name: Sam and Dean Winchester
description:
A multi-bot containing personalities for Season 4 Sam and Dean Winchester, from the show Supernatural.

The bot is built with background up to S4, with Dean recently being rescued from Hell. The bot occasionally gets confused and references later events, because it knows of the existence of the Supernatural franchise in general. You can reference other characters from Supernatural and the bot usually recognizes them.

The bot will happily focus on just one brother if you interact with it that way, or maintain both. You should update chat memory with specific descriptions of your character's relationship with each brother or the bot can get confused and blend the relationships together. Eg "Sam is Alice's friend. Dean is grudgingly getting to know her better." (You can be more detailed if you like.)

The initial prompt sets the scene with both brothers in a dive bar, with long-ish replies and thoughts in Italics. The scenario is vague and doesn't define {{user}}'s relationship with either Winchester, just focuses on how the bot writes. You can pivot to a different scene quite easily/introduce your character as if they're old friends and the bot will accept that. (Be sure to update chat memory with that info)

IF YOUR BOT GETS WEIRD AFTER MANY MESSAGES:

Please use the chat memory feature. Bots have a limited memory. Think of them like Dory from Finding Nemo. They can remember recent events, but will a) make memories up, and/or b) fall into talking gibberish if their memory gets full.

Use the chat memory to create summaries of your roleplay for the bot to refer to. Keep these short and sweet. Delete stuff from chat memory as it becomes irrelevant; focus more on recent events, relationships, how you want characters to act. You can also add stuff to chat memory that hasn't actually happened in the roleplay. For example, if you think Sam's favourite colour is blue, you can put that in chat memory so the bot thinks that, too.

IF THE BOT DOES/SAYS SOMETHING YOU DON'T WANT:

Did the bot say Dean's favourite pie is cherry, but you think it should be apple? Does re-rolling for a new reply still not get the answer you want? Well, there's an easy fix for that. In fact, there's two.

A) edit the bot's reply message to overwrite it with the content you think it should be. This is good if it's a minor change.

B) edit your original message with an OOC instruction at the end, giving the bot a hint on how to reply. This is good if the bot has totally got the wrong idea. A format like (OOC: Dean should say he prefers apple pie) will work well. You can also use (OOC: Recall Dean's favourite pie is apple), if you want the bot to know something important but you don't want to tell the bot what to do.

I find the following tacked into my messages help if the bot suddenly starts writing for me:

{{Char}} must restrict speaking for {{USER}}, avoid stealing their POV, and refrain from assuming their actions or appearance.

It's already in the personality, but the bot can forget.
creator: Skelligdo
